name: Circular by Piedrabuena and the Tabla de la Yedra (Río Bullaque) — from Porzuna
name_original: Circular por Piedrabuena y la Tabla de la Yedra (Río Bullaque) — desde Porzuna
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Porzuna are situated in the province of Ciudad Real, within the Castilla-La Mancha region of Spain. This area is characterized by the Montes de Toledo and the Sierra Morena, offering varied terrain from plains to rugged mountains and ridges. The landscape includes unique volcanic features, such as the Volcán del Cerro Santo, and river valleys like the Bullaque. Cyclists can expect roads with varied elevation and scenic viewpoints across these natural features. Best road cycling routes around Porzuna The most popular road cycling route is Picón y La Tabla de la Yedra desde Ciudad Real – circular por Piedrabuena y Alcolea de Calatrava, a 73.3 miles (118.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a comprehensive loop through the region, connecting several towns and natural areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop, a moderate 31.5 miles (50.6 km) path. This route features a notable climb at Puerto de los Robledillos, providing panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Malagón and Porzuna Circular Route, a 58.3 miles (93.8 km) trail leading through the Montes de Toledo region, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Road cycling around Porzuna is defined by rolling hills, volcanic landscapes, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 184 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Porzuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Porzuna?
A: There are 20 road cycling routes around Porzuna listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences. These include 2 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Porzuna?
A: Road cycling routes around Porzuna offer diverse terrain, from plains with rich seasonal grasslands to rugged mountains and ridges within the Montes de Toledo and Sierra Morena regions. You'll encounter varied elevation, including rolling hills and challenging ascents, alongside river valleys like the Bullaque.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Porzuna?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092411523/malagon-and-porzuna-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malagón and Porzuna Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a 93.8 km moderate trail through the Montes de Toledo. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366186240/circular-by-piedrabuena-and-the-tabla-de-la-yedra-rio-bullaque-from-porzuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular by Piedrabuena and the Tabla de la Yedra (Río Bullaque) — from Porzuna&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.8 km moderate loop.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks can I see while road cycling in Porzuna?
A: The region offers numerous scenic spots. You can cycle near unique volcanic features like the Volcán del Cerro Santo, which provides panoramic views, or the Laguna Volcánica de Alcolea. Routes also pass through river valleys, such as along the Bullaque River, and offer views of various Sierras including Cerrajón and Miraflores. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3865555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt; is a notable river highlight.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. There are 5 difficult road cycling routes around Porzuna. A prominent example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084185016/picon-y-la-tabla-de-la-yedra-desde-ciudad-real-circular-por-piedrabuena-y-alcolea-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón y La Tabla de la Yedra desde Ciudad Real – circular por Piedrabuena y Alcolea de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, a 118 km route with over 930 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067730682/cabaneros-national-park-and-torre-de-abraham-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 121.5 km ride with significant climbs.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling around Porzuna?
A: The roads in the Montes de Toledo region, where Porzuna is located, are generally in good condition with minimal traffic. This provides cyclists with quiet routes and smooth surfaces, ideal for both training and leisurely rides. Many routes combine well-paved sections with varied elevation.

Q: Can I cycle near natural parks or reserves in the Porzuna area?
A: Yes, Porzuna's location provides access to significant protected areas. You can find routes that take you close to the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, known for its biodiversity, or the Reserva Natural Las Navas de Malagón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067730682/cabaneros-national-park-and-torre-de-abraham-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a specific route that explores this area.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy road cycling routes around Porzuna. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, these easier options would be suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your group's ability.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Porzuna?
A: The region experiences varied weather. Generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons provide comfortable riding conditions to enjoy the scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable climbs or ascents for road cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, the Montes de Toledo region features several notable climbs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366194974/los-cortijos-and-puerto-de-los-robledillos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes a significant ascent at Puerto de los Robledillos, offering panoramic views. The diverse terrain ensures options for cyclists seeking elevation challenges.

Q: What kind of natural monuments can I discover near Porzuna while cycling?
A: Beyond the volcanic features, you can find other natural monuments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6772678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Teja Spring&lt;/a&gt; is one such natural highlight. The region's river valleys and various Sierras also contribute to its natural beauty, offering diverse landscapes to explore.

Located in the Maiyor square next to the Town Hall. It is a Neoclassical style construction. Among the construction materials, brick predominates, and of course volcanic stone, and the metallic roofs of the towers stand out. Very nice in general everything.

As it passes through Piedrabuena, the Bullaque River has an area of calm waters known as the Tabla de la Yedra. This is a place of recreation that is visited annually by thousands of people, being one of the most outstanding spaces equipped for bathing in the province of Ciudad Real.

Porzuna was a Roman and Visigoth settlement, and it is from these civilizations that historical remains have been found. The first settlers of the region are probably from the Lower and Middle Paleolithic according to the archaeological remains found. Most of these can be found in the Ciudad Real museum.

waypoints.name: Tabla de la Yedra Recreation Area
